description: `@alga-psa/workflows` is the canonical module root for workflow-owned code after the cutover.
The EE workflows package exports deep entrypoints for runtime, workers, persistence, bundle, streams, expression-authoring, secrets, services, and types that cover current caller needs.
The EE workflows package build configuration emits the expanded workflow entrypoints required by server, worker, and package consumers.
TypeScript path aliases in root, server, EE server, and workflow worker configs resolve the EE workflows package as the canonical workflow namespace.
Workflow runtime bootstrap is owned and invoked through the EE workflows package rather than a shared workflow namespace.
Workflow worker entrypoints import runtime and worker helpers exclusively from `@alga-psa/workflows`.
EE workflow server actions import workflow runtime, validation, persistence, bundle, and catalog helpers exclusively from `@alga-psa/workflows`.
Workflow designer and related EE UI code import workflow client, catalog, expression, and AI helpers exclusively from `@alga-psa/workflows`.
Workflow persistence models and interfaces are owned by the EE workflows package and remain available to existing workflow callers.
Workflow bundle helpers and types are owned by the EE workflows package and remain behaviorally compatible.
Workflow workers are owned by the EE workflows package and keep current startup and run-processing behavior.
Workflow streams and domain-event builder helpers are owned by the EE workflows package and keep current signatures and payload behavior.
Workflow-specific expression-authoring helpers are owned by the EE workflows package and continue to support existing UI/editor consumers.
Workflow-specific secrets and services are owned by the EE workflows package and remain available to existing runtime and integration callers.
Workflow client and type surfaces are owned by the EE workflows package and preserve existing consumer contracts.
AI schema helpers are owned by the EE workflows package rather than a shared workflow path.
AI workflow action registration is owned by the EE workflows package rather than a shared workflow path.
AI-aware action output schema resolution is owned by the EE workflows package rather than a shared workflow path.
AI-aware workflow publish validation is owned by the EE workflows package rather than a shared workflow path.
Shared/core workflow code no longer imports EE-only AI services after the cutover.
Server code outside the EE workflows package that invokes workflow-owned runtime or persistence helpers imports them from `@alga-psa/workflows`.
Packages outside the workflow module that emit workflow-owned domain events import those helpers from `@alga-psa/workflows/streams/...`.
Package manifests are updated anywhere a direct `@alga-psa/workflows` dependency becomes required by the import move.
Workflow-related wiring tests and source-string assertions are updated to the EE workflows package import paths.
No non-doc imports remain from `@shared/workflow*` after the hard cut.
No non-doc imports remain from `@alga-psa/shared/workflow*` after the hard cut.
Workflow runtime execution semantics remain unchanged after the ownership move.
Workflow worker startup, event ingestion, and run-processing behavior remain unchanged after the ownership move.
Workflow bundle import/export behavior remains unchanged after the ownership move.
Workflow designer behavior remains unchanged after the ownership move.
Workflow task inbox and workflow persistence consumers remain behaviorally unchanged after the ownership move.
Workflow streams and event publishing behavior remain unchanged after the ownership move.
AI workflow step behavior remains unchanged while moving AI ownership fully into the EE workflows package.
The codebase no longer places workflow implementation ownership under the global shared namespace after the cutover lands.
